nodes.name: 開曼群島
nodes.summary: 開曼群島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联12种鸟类，包括Caribbean dove、White-crowned pigeon、北撲翅鴷等12种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 舍布魯克
nodes.summary: 舍布魯克是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括北撲翅鴷，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 北撲翅鴷
nodes.summary: 北撲翅鴷（Northern flicker，学名 Colaptes auratus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于開曼群島、舍布魯克、下加利福尼亞半島。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is native to most of North America, parts of Central America, Cuba, and the Cayman Islands, and is one of the few woodpecker species that migrate

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |date=2023-07-07 |title=Northern Flicker (Colaptes auratus), version 2.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld-org.proxy3.library.mcgill.ca/bow/species/norfli/cur/movement#migroute |access-date=2023-10-11 |website=Birds of the World}} Individuals that breed farther north travel greater distances than their migratory southern conspecifics, often resulting in the convergence of northern and southern populations at wintering sites. This discrepancy likely arises from the species' ground foraging behavior, where prey can only be found in snow-free locations. Furthermore, females tend to winter farther north than males, suggesting that parental investment and division of reproductive labour are key factors in determining individual migratory behavior. Rising temperatures have been shown to trigger migration prematurely in northern flickers as well as many other migratory bird species, as evidenced in Sherbrooke, Quebec.{{Cite journal |last1=Jones |first1=Norman K

links.key: bird-北撲翅鴷-73045ab3__distributed_in__loc-下加利福尼亞半島-4e2a0f71
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: states winter in Texas, Oklahoma, and Arkansas. Those breeding from southern Ontario and Michigan to New England winter from eastern Texas to the Carolinas, whereas those breeding in British Columbia and the Pacific Northwest winter from central California to the Baja California peninsula in Mexico. During migration, northern flickers may form flocks.{{Cite journal |last1=Gow |first1=Elizabeth A
